text:
Section 37 report

It appears to the court that it may be appropriate for a care or supervision order to be made with respect to the child[ren] because [insert reasons].

[Name] Council must undertake an investigation of the child[ren]’s circumstances under section 37 of the Children Act 1989 and consider whether they should apply for a care or supervision order, provide services as assistance for the child[ren] or the family or take any other action in respect of the child[ren].

The court must send this order to the legal adviser to the local authority by [date] together with the following documents: [insert].

If the local authority decides not to apply for a care or supervision order they must by 4.00pm on [date (eight weeks)] file at court a report setting out

the reasons for so deciding;

any services or assistance they have provided, or intend to provide, for the child[ren] and the family;

any other action they have taken, or propose to take, with respect to the child[ren]; and

when they propose to review the case.

Upon receipt of the report the court will consider whether it should be sent to the parties. It will, in any event, be considered at the next hearing.

Section 91(14) Order

Pursuant to section 91(14) of the Children Act 1989 no application in respect of the child[ren] for [a child arrangements order] / [a specific issue order] / [a prohibited steps order] / [permission to cause the child to be known by a new surname] / [permission to remove the child from the United Kingdom] / [insert other type of order] / [an order under any [other] provision of the Children Act 1989] / [, [and] variation or discharge of those orders] / [,and enforcement of those orders] shall be made by [name] without the court’s permission until [time and date] / [until [the youngest] child attains the age of 16] / [further order].

Any application for permission to apply shall, in the first instance, not be served on the respondent(s) and shall be considered on the papers. If the court is satisfied that the application shows sufficient merit, the application for permission to apply shall be served on the respondents and sted for an on notice hearing.
